### Task: refactor messy cards + build `styles.css` You are given **broken** HTML below. It uses **inline styles** and **wrong** use of `id`. Your job is to **fix it** and supply a **separate** stylesheet. **Requirements (checklist — your solution must satisfy all):** 1. Remove **all** `style="..."` attributes from the three boxes. 2. Use **one class** named **`card`** on all three `
`s (same class, not the same `id`). 3. Only **one** `
` may keep a **unique** `id`: change the middle box to `id="featured-card"` (the other two have **no** `id`). 4. Create **`styles.css`** linked with `` inside ``. 5. In **`styles.css`**, style **`.card`** so every card has: - `background-color: #e3f2fd;` - `padding: 18px;` - `margin-bottom: 14px;` - `border: 2px solid #1565c0;` - `max-width: 320px;` 6. Style **`#featured-card`** so **only the middle** card gets **`border: 4px solid #ff6f00;`** (keep other `.card` rules still applying where they don’t conflict — you may override only the border width/color on the featured one). **Starting HTML (copy and fix this):** ```html Cards
